title: Limit Container Memory
description:
## Cap a Container's Resources

Without limits a container can consume all of the host's memory. Set an explicit cap.

**Requirements:**
1. Run an **`alpine`** container named **`limited`** in the background (keep it alive)
2. Limit it to **64 MB** of memory (`--memory=64m`)

> 💡 `docker run -d --name limited --memory=64m alpine sleep 3600`. Docker stores this as bytes; 64m = 67108864 bytes.

hints.title: Apply a memory limit
hints.body: The --memory flag accepts suffixes like m (megabytes) and g (gigabytes).

hints.title: Inspect the limit
hints.body: The effective limit is reported in bytes under HostConfig.Memory.

validation.description: Checks that 'limited' is running with a 64 MB (67108864 byte) memory cap.
